statement.p: Solve for x in the inequality \frac{4}{7}-\frac{4}{3}x\le\frac{2}{3}\text{.} Write the solution set in both set-builder notation and interval notation.

solution.p: The LCM of the denominators is 21\text{,} so we start out by multiplying each side of the inequality by 21\text{.}

Note that when we divided each side of the inequality by -28\text{,} the inequality symbol reversed direction. The solution set in set-builder notation is \left\{x\mid x\ge-\frac{1}{14}\right\}\text{.} The solution set in interval notation is \left[-\frac{1}{14},\infty\right)\text{.}
